reviews.review_title: I hate this game SO much.
reviews.review_description:
For its lack of clear instructions when going from goal to goal.

For going back and forth this huge map without teleportation redoing the same challenges over and over again. 

For the worst humor and voice acting I’d ever heard 

I didn’t refund it because the beginning is great, but as the map gets larger, the game gets lamer.

This game frustrated me so that I straight up absolutely hate it. 

Still working on finishing it as I’m over two hours in and it’s hard for me to delete all the progress I’ve made. I do however totally regret having ever started this nonsense game.

Hate it!

If you want to waste your money, here’s a link below, though there are other, better games on that link. 

Cheers 

L I N K T R , 
                    E E
                          /
                            D I S C O U N T
                                                  F U R Y

So I’m still playing the game right and so far it’s a little fun but kinda repetitive and it makes you go back a forth and back and forth just to push a button I liked the first game but this one is also a little boring at times and I hate that they like when you first enter the ship they just made it a little video instead of you actually entering it (if that makes any sense) and it feels a little lazy 

And it won’t let me refund the game

This game is amazing. The controls, mechanics, puzzels, movement and artstyle are perfect. It gives you a true "spiderman" feeling and it feels amazing to fly through the whole ship and fight enemies. The puzzles are fun and not too hard. The sense of humor in this game is very dry and made me laugh several times. But...

I finished the campaign twice and it gets kind of repetitive fast. The only thing that is missing is some kind of replayable mode, where I can get back after finishing the game.

As a former Yupitergrad 1 player and admirer, the only thing that I would love to see is Time Attack (like YG1) with some sort of level editor which gives you endless replayability.  Where you can make your own levels or play community made levels and beat the fastest time. That would be awesome and make the replayability endless.

Or some kind of "endless" mode where you swing through an infinite randomly generated space with only 3 lives. With pick-ups like a shield, weapon upgrades, health etc. Here you can het points by shooting enemies or getting the furthest without dying.

I would love to hear if there will be a future update which will have new game mode(s)
If this would be added, then this is the perfect game.

Other than that still 5/5 stars and an excellent game!
YUPITERGRAD is awesome and I love it but you need to add the extra game modes from the 1st game maybe even add a battle royal mode

reviews.review_title: Fun - but a bit sickening for me
reviews.review_description:
Loved yupitergrad 1, played it with no problems, can complete the game in one sitting and having fun with the time trials. 

Yupitergrad 2 after 30 minutes I'm getting VR sick pretty consistently, which is such a shame. 

Mini spoiler:
No idea how much is left of the game, just got another gun upgrade and can shoot things, but am putting this on hold for now.

The gameplay will almost be the same from the beginning to the end albeit some new accessories that only make it more cumbersome rather than rich.

Your goal is just to go from one point to the other on a giant and empty map, going back and forth in corridors your already explored.

Everything feels like a cheap way to extend the play through.

How frustrating given how much I enjoyed the first Yupitergrad. I have a hard time believing that this game fairly earned a 4.5/5 rating.

Mild spoiler:

The ending sucks, the boss is easier to kill than some regular enemies. Then after a dozen more long and boring corridors, you board a ship to leave the station. But rather than having you actually leave the station in the ship, the screen turns black and a cartoon is played instead. 

That’s is beyond lazy and cheap for 30$. What a rip off.

developer_response.body: Hi there - we are sorry that the game wasn't up to par with your expectations, especially after giving the first game such a high rating. We will try to improve upon the game constantly with new updates and game modes, so hopefully in the future you may come back to the game and have a better time at it. Thanks for your honest feedback!

reviews.review_description: I played the first Yupitergrad and it quickly became my favorite game of all time. I was worried about the sequel, I felt it had a LOT to live up to, and at first I wasn't a fan of the gun mechanics I saw in the trailer. But it blew my expectations out of the water. The swinging and level design is better than ever, and AIsha is a great companion through the game. The gun mechanics ended up being perfectly executed, making for an exhilarating addition to the game. This sequel literally could not have been better. Thank you gamedust!

developer_response.body: This is very high praise, for Yupitergrad 2 to meet the expectations of your favourite game :D Great to hear you are enjoying the game as much - we will continue working diligently to make the game even better with patches and other game modes so stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: I hope you like getting lost…
reviews.review_description:
The first couple of hours were that of a five star game that built upon the extraordinary foundation of Yupitergrad. The last few hours, however, I have spent getting lost down long tunnels with no shortcuts between them, backtracking, and getting lost again. 

I just spent 20m traversing from Docks to Fuel Tanks trying to get to life support, only to discover some sort of bug makes me get stuck in a loop between save points and I have to backtrack across the entire map and take the OTHER insanely long path to Life Support. I just HOPE that works.  

Navigation kills this game. I get lost literally every time I try to get somewhere. EVEN FOLLOWING WALKTHROUGHS. I can’t tell if my struggles are bugs or bad design, and that is damning.

The map has overlapping tunnels that make it appear are contiguous but are in fact dead ends, the “signpost” labels on doors are totally unhelpful and often contradictory, and mission directions will be impossible to complete without some intermediate waypoint that required you to loop completely across the map the other way without specifying—then the next mission is basically just “go back the whole way you just came”. Which you’ll do at least three times because you got lost again.

You’re constantly passing doors you can’t unlock yet for the first few hoursso it’s never clear when “next” route you should take. 

I went from loving this game to wishing I’d just replayed Yupitergrad.

Brilliant concept laid low by what I can only imagine is a complete absence of outside play testing. It seems like somebody bought another company’s nearly finished game and just hit publish without looking at it.

It could have been great. I don’t know that it’s fixable.

The sad thing is, you should still probably buy it for the first half. The real cost is the time you won’t get back from the interminable second if you’re a completionist.

You can have similar hours of fun by rolling a chair up and down the same hallway

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: Hi there again, we have released a new Cosmic Clarity update a few weeks back which should make the navigation throughout the space station much easier now :) 

Thanks for your extensive feedback, our team has identified the issue and is currently working on a solution to fix it. We apologize for any inconvenience it may have caused you. 

+We understand the importance of accurate navigation and we assure you that we are actively addressing this issue as well. Our team is committed to improving our product and providing a better user experience. We value your support and patience during this process.

We wanted Yupitergrad 2 to be the truest sequel experience and we will continue to improve upon it with patches, so stay tuned!.

reviews.review_description: This game needs markers where to go on the map instead of just objectives

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks a lot for your feedback, we will think about it - even though the main story of Yupitergrad 2: The Lost Station is over, we will try to continue working on it with other content and quality of life updates :) So stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: Sequel that opens up the Space
reviews.review_description: Cool sequel to Yupitergrad that not only upgrades the Player's repertoire (guns!) but also opens up the space station to free exploration (comes with a handy 3D map). Lots of fun gravity swinging with multi-quest paths ensues - would be almost a breeze if the owners didn't install devices that can easily transform the Player into chopped meat...

developer_response.body: We are so glad you enjoyed the game, stay tuned for future updates :)

reviews.review_title: Physics were cool, took too long to make
reviews.review_description: Physics were super cool (ish). took too long to make it through the intro. Didn’t even get to shoot anything, which, allegedly, is a thing? - (ish)because you labeled it a zero g game, when it was clearly not?

developer_response.body: Thank you for your honest review! So sorry that the intro felt too long to you - we can assure you that you were very close to the actual shooting stuff in the game, so I hope you will find some time to give the game another chance! Also, there is for sure some Zero Gravity action later down the line :)

reviews.review_description: Good game, hard to navigate, but fun overall

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks a lot for your review and feedback :)

reviews.review_title: One of the most original games on Quest!
reviews.review_description: The movement mechanics are great... and they give you bonus arm-strengthening exercises ;) The feeling of being in the space is really intensive and the soundtrack just turns up the vibe. Haven't played Yupitergrad 1, but will definetely give it a try as well!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your feedback, we really strived for a unique but satisfying VR experience! Also, thanks for the praise on the soundtrack, we really appreciate that <3

reviews.review_description:
This game takes everything good about yupitergrad, like puzzles, mechanics, level design, graphics, animations, etc. And multiples the budget by like 10! 

First off, I want to talk about the combat. So, in the sequel, you will be fending off against evil robots. To rebel against them, you will use two different weapons. A one-shot explosion gun and a minigun. The minigun has a way faster fire rate but does less damage and the same thing, but flip floped for the other weapon. The combat is fun but can get a little repetitive at times.

Next on the list is level design! So this games level design is basically more yupitergrad. Most of the levels have identical level design ideas. But there are some levels that are just huge arenas that you fight in. There's also puzzle levels. 

Open World! This game is basically an open world game! It'll let you go wherever you want to do missions. You have to find ways around. And unlock more of the map. This will leave you finding secrets and revisited rooms multiple times throughout the campaign.

New Level Themes! The new level themes include a lava theme, a construction theme, a cargo theme, a plant theme, and more!

Easy 9.5/10 for me definitely a must buy!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your extensive feedback and all the words of overwhelming support! We are so glad that you enjoyed the game to this full extent - stay tuned for more updates and game modes later down the line :)

reviews.review_title: Great time, wish there were more
reviews.review_description:
Overall the movement in this game is extremely excellent and compelling. Combat can seem daunting at first, but by the end you'll feel like a superhero. 

I just wish there were more content to it. I'm giving it a 4 and highly recommend it, but I'll gladly give it a 5 if the devs ever add time trials (or some kind of arena mode)
Made it to combat room byt guns disappeared. Reset progress and reinstalled. Now only 1 gun available. Seems ti be a glitch that makes game unplayable.

Ok this game is nuts !I mean first yupitergrad blew me away but this…..this is pure gold.Probably my best VR experience.It doesn’t get better than this.
Being a huge metroid fan, I felt home….this is the closest to what a metroid in VR would be.

The physics are incredible.Once you get used to the controls, mooving around almost feels unreal.

I had several moments during my playthrough while I thought « ok this game is on another level » 
I had a real BLAST .
This game should be on every top 10 games list,easily.

Kudos to the devs ! What you’ve done is a masterclass !
Thanks!

developer_response.body: We're delighted whenever someone recognizes our effort to bring the Metroidvania experience to VR. Thank you! <3

reviews.review_description: looks good and plays good but just a little boring

The strengths of this game include action, puzzles, and adventure.
But in my opinion, the best thing about this game is the humor that runs throughout it.

-Picture style
-Plunger swing
-Equipment that gets upgraded as time goes on (you also get a gun)
-Lovely AI that talks non-stop
-Strange obstacle enough to kill me (don't ask me why it's there)
-Huge, bizarre, yet humorous structures that appear as you pass through a corridor filled with obstacles, etc.

I hope you experience this game right away. I am sure you will fall in love with this game.

Be careful...if you are directionless, you may have a bit of trouble!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback! We have released a Cosmic Clarity update that should makes the navigation a little bit better also :)

reviews.review_description: It starts slow to get you adjusted. By the end your swinging around like Spider-Man with a gun. It is an amazing game from start to finish . The backtracking can be a little annoying but by the time you have to backtrack you have developed your skills so it’s easy. This and the first episode I see as a list buy.  I put this in the high quality category like budget cuts and to the top(amazing ). Please make more like this.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review!
